fundamentals.name: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. engages in the design, manufacture, and sale of fiber-optic networking products in the United States, Taiwan, and China. The company offers optical modules, optical filters, lasers, laser components, subassemblies, transmitters and transceivers, turn-key equipment, headend, node, and distribution equipment, as well as amplifiers. It sells its products to internet data center operators, cable television, telecom equipment manufacturers, fiber-to-the-home, and internet service providers through its direct and indirect sales channels. Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. was incorporated in 1997 and is headquartered in Sugar Land, Texas.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing revenue but unprofitable, with a high forward P/E and significant debt.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed estimates with EPS of -0.07 vs -0.05; next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 51.4% YoY — exceptional top-line expansion.
Analyst growth est, current FY 441.7% — massive expected earnings growth.
PEG ratio 0.78 — suggests growth may be priced in, but still attractive if estimates hold.
Current ratio 3.83 and Quick ratio 2.85 — strong short-term liquidity.
Debt/Equity 25.36 is high, but total debt is less than total cash.
negatives: Operating margin -8.6% and Profit margin -8.5% — the company is losing money.
Return on equity -6.1% and Return on assets -3.3% — poor profitability.
Free cash flow (TTM) $-445.58M — burning cash significantly.
Forward P/E 37.68 — valuation is high given current unprofitability.
Last quarter EPS surprise -44.8% — missed estimates significantly.

news.paragraph: Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) has seen a dramatic surge in its stock price, with articles highlighting a potential "bubble or bottleneck" scenario and questioning if the upside is already priced in, despite significant transceiver orders and AI-related growth. While the company has experienced substantial gains over the past six months, concerns linger about its profitability and whether the current valuation is sustainable. The upcoming earnings report will be crucial for assessing the company's trajectory.
